name: Talaia d'Alcudia and Penya des Migdia - circular trail running on the Alcudia peninsula
name_original: Talaia d'Alcúdia y Penya des Migdia — circular trailrunning por la península de Alcúdia
region_intro: Jogging around Alcúdia offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths to challenging mountainous trails. The region is characterized by extensive sandy beaches, the La Victoria peninsula with its significant elevation gains, and protected wetland areas like S'Albufera Natural Park. This variety provides options for running through wooded areas, along inland paths, or up inclines, all embraced by the Mediterranean Sea. Best jogging routes around Alcúdia The most popular jogging route is Talaia d'Alcudia and Penya des Migdia - circular trail running on the Alcudia peninsula, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views over the bay. Another top favourite among local runners is Alcúdia Beach Promenade – Port of Alcúdia loop from Protected Area: Estany des Ponts, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route follows a flat coastal promenade, ideal for leisurely runs with sea views. Local runners also love the View of Port de Pollença – Port de Pollença beach loop from Can Capdebou (Alcúdia), a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Jogging around Alcúdia is defined by coastal paths, mountainous trails of the La Victoria peninsula, and flat wetland areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy beach runs to difficult ascents. The routes in Alcúdia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 24000 runners have used komoot to explore Alcúdia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running terrain can I expect in Alcúdia?
A: Alcúdia offers a diverse range of running terrain. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;flat coastal paths&lt;/strong&gt; along extensive sandy beaches like Platja d'Alcúdia and Platja de Muro, ideal for leisurely jogs. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;strong&gt;La Victoria peninsula&lt;/strong&gt; provides trails with significant elevation gain and panoramic sea views. Additionally, S'Albufera Natural Park offers &lt;strong&gt;flat, serene paths&lt;/strong&gt; through wetlands, and there are routes through wooded areas and orchards.

Q: Are there many running routes available in Alcúdia?
A: Yes, Alcúdia is a fantastic destination for runners. There are &lt;strong&gt;over 190 dedicated jogging routes&lt;/strong&gt; in the area, catering to various fitness levels. Approximately 34 of these are considered easy, 116 moderate, and 46 difficult, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced trail runners.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging routes in Alcúdia?
A: Absolutely. The flat coastal paths, such as those along Platja d'Alcúdia and Platja de Muro, are excellent for family jogs. These routes are generally easy and accessible, providing a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. The S'Albufera Natural Park also offers flat, scenic paths suitable for families.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alcúdia?
A: The running routes in Alcúdia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.3 stars from over 340 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Runners often praise the incredible variety of terrain, from stunning coastal views to challenging mountain trails, and the opportunity to explore the region's natural beauty. Over 24,000 runners have used komoot to discover these diverse paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alcúdia?
A: Yes, many routes in Alcúdia are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example for experienced runners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000802714/talaia-d-alcudia-and-penya-des-migdia-circular-trail-running-on-the-alcudia-peninsula" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia d'Alcudia and Penya des Migdia - circular trail running on the Alcudia peninsula&lt;/a&gt;, which offers challenging climbs and breathtaking panoramic views over the bay.

Q: What are some scenic landmarks or natural features I can see while running in Alcúdia?
A: Alcúdia's routes are rich with scenic beauty. Along coastal paths, you'll enjoy extensive sandy beaches and sea views. On the La Victoria peninsula, you'll encounter panoramic vistas of the Badia de Pollença and Cap Formentor. The S'Albufera Natural Park offers serene wetland landscapes. You can also explore historical sites like the walled Old Town of Alcúdia and Roman remains, or discover natural highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/307283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de Sementer&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/229054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrent de les Agulles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners?
A: Certainly. For beginners or those seeking a relaxed run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Alcúdia – Alcúdia Beach Promenade loop from Protected Area: Estany des Ponts&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This moderate 6.1-mile (9.8 km) path follows a flat coastal promenade, perfect for leisurely runs with beautiful sea views and minimal elevation.

Q: What is the best season for running in Alcúdia?
A: Alcúdia's mild and temperate climate makes it suitable for running year-round. However, &lt;strong&gt;spring is particularly ideal&lt;/strong&gt; due to the vibrant natural scenery and comfortable temperatures. Early mornings or late evenings are often recommended during the warmer summer months to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Port de Pollença?
A: Yes, there is a popular route that takes you towards Port de Pollença. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23832276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Port de Pollença – Port de Pollença beach loop from Can Capdebou (Alcúdia)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.0-mile (9.7 km) trail that leads through coastal areas, offering scenic views of the port.

Q: Can I find an athletics track in Alcúdia for training?
A: Yes, Alcúdia has an athletics track. You can find a 400-meter athletics track (pista de atletismo) in Alcúdia, which is suitable for speed work and structured training sessions.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a sunrise run?
A: Absolutely. The coastal areas are perfect for a sunrise run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31551643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sunrise at Alcúdia Beach – Alcúdia Beach Walk loop from Alcúdia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6-mile (13.9 km) route that offers stunning views as the sun rises over the Mediterranean, providing a beautiful start to your day.

Q: What kind of elevation can I expect on Alcúdia's running trails?
A: Elevation varies significantly across Alcúdia's running trails. You can find very flat routes, such as the coastal promenades with minimal elevation gain (e.g., less than 20 meters). Conversely, challenging trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000802714/talaia-d-alcudia-and-penya-des-migdia-circular-trail-running-on-the-alcudia-peninsula" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia d'Alcudia and Penya des Migdia circular trail&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial climbs, with over 500 meters of elevation gain, offering a rigorous workout and rewarding views.
tips: At the end of the highway on the peninsula Victoria stands the old monastery Ermita de la Victoria. On the plaza in the shadow of the huge pine trees you have a great view of the bay of Pollença and the peninsula Formentor. The former convent now houses simple and very nice hotel rooms. From the parking lot at the monastery start various hikes across the peninsula.

At the crossroads where you can descend to the beach Platja des Col Baix, there is a well-kept rest area with shelter and a simple toilet. The wooden benches stand in the shade of large pine trees and you have a beautiful view of the bay with the steep cliffs.

Accessible via several routes. Coming from the south, it's much more scenic, but also more strenuous.

The tin hut is often occupied and serves as a fire watchtower.

Great views over the Badia de Pollença, Puig Romaní, Cap Formentor, the Penya Roja, the northern foothills of the Tramuntana, the Badia d'Alcúdia and its resorts, and even Cap de Ferrutx.


If approaching from the south, it should be easier to find the way forward from the summit.

Great view to the Cap Formentor, over the Cap Pinar and the Puig Romaní.
The last piece is a bit steep and screeching.

waypoints.name: Penya des Migdia Summit and Cannon Viewpoint
